title: Substack vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Drip: pricing, features, and which email marketing tool suits your needs best.
h1: Substack vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Substack and Drip serve fundamentally different purposes in the email marketing landscape. Substack is designed for writers and creators to build paid newsletters directly, while Drip specializes in e-commerce email automation and revenue tracking. Your choice depends on whether you're monetizing content or running an online store.

comparison_table.tool_a: Free with revenue-share (10% on paid subscriptions)

comparison_table.tool_b: No free tier; limits depend on paid plan
E-commerce businesses with product tracking needs

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, recommendations
comparison_table.tool_b: E-commerce tracking, SMS, visual automation, revenue attribution, segmentation

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator wanting to launch a paid newsletter with zero upfront cost
You want access to Substack's built-in audience discovery features and recommendations network
You plan to monetize through direct reader subscriptions rather than product sales
choose_b_if: You run an e-commerce store and need deep integration with your product catalog
You require advanced automation, SMS marketing, and revenue attribution tracking
You're willing to pay for a dedicated platform with stronger overall ratings and e-commerce focus

faq.a: Neither is universally better—they serve different markets. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing newsletters, while Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses needing automated product-based marketing. Your choice depends entirely on your business model and goals.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Drip?
faq.a: Substack is free to start with no upfront costs; you only pay 10% commission on paid subscriptions you generate. Drip requires a minimum $39/month investment. For bootstrapped creators, Substack's free tier is significantly cheaper initially.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your subscriber list from Drip and import it to Substack, though some platform-specific features (like e-commerce tracking) won't transfer. The switch is straightforward for basic email lists, but you'll lose Drip's automation and e-commerce functionality.
